text: The surgeon performs a left video-assisted thoracoscopic thymectomy, inserting trocars under direct guidance. The pleura beneath the sternum is opened using cautery, followed by a combination of blunt and sharp dissection to separate the thymus tissue.

The surgeon uses scissors to divide the pleura near the phrenic nerve, revealing the junction of the mammary and innominate vein. The innominate vein is clearly visible. Blunt dissection is employed to separate the thymus from the pericardium.

The surgeon dissects and divides the thymic veins draining into the innominate vein using a ligature. The retroinnominate portion of the thymus is carefully dissected, including the left superior horn, which is separated from the great vessels. The right superior horn is then addressed, with an extension of thymic tissue seen overlying the innominate vein.

The surgeon dissects additional portions of the cervical thymus, dividing fascial attachments to free the gland. The freed thymus is placed in a retrieval bag. A hole is created in the pleura to manage the contralateral capnothorax, and the bag containing the thymus is extracted. Intercostal blocks are placed, and trochars are removed while performing a Valsalva maneuver.
